ratings.comment: Amazing woman. Super accommodating. Don't listen to the other reviews. Obviously you shouldn't take a class you're not interested in or think will be an easy A and complain about it. The content is so interesting and she is the sweetest woman who genuinely cares about her students and their learning.

Overall Laurel was a very nice woman and always accessible outside and during office hours. She wasn't always the best at explaining advanced topics, but oftentimes the textbook could cover it better. My biggest complaint is that a good chunk of her exam questions (probably about half) were overly specific or tough. It's a lot of information.
If you don't like geology do not take this class. Very boring. The prof is very unclear and grades harshly. Her exams are harder than they should be.
Don't mind the 106 naysayers, she does a fine job.

Laurel is one of the nicest people i have ever met. she's very helpful and willing to talk about anything with you. she really wants her students to succeed. 202 was a difficult class, but required for geol major. she does dumb it down sometimes but that can be helpful too. laurel rules. structural geology does not.
